source_papers.title: Functional recovery of the adult murine hippocampus after cryopreservation by vitrification
Restoration of ovarian function and natural fertility following cryopreservation and autotransplantation of whole adult sheep ovaries
Supercooling enables long-term transplantation survival following 4 days of liver preservation
Validation of a high-throughput screening assay for CPA toxicity characterisation

extraction_metadata.extraction_notes: Additional formulations and protocols from various organ/tissue-specific papers. These fill out the knowledge base beyond the three major cocktails.

formulations.name: VM3 (brain vitrification)
formulations.context: Used in recent hippocampal vitrification studies

biological_outcomes.outcome: Metabolic recovery and electrophysiological function demonstrated post-vitrification. Synaptic activity partially preserved.
biological_outcomes.notes: One of the first demonstrations of functional neural tissue recovery after vitrification in adult mammalian brain

formulations.confidence_note: VM3 composition details are approximate — extracted from preprint which may not report full formulation details. Verify against final publication.

formulations.name: Slow-freeze glycerol protocol (ovarian tissue)
formulations.context: Traditional slow-freezing approach for whole ovary preservation

biological_outcomes.outcome: Restoration of ovarian function and natural fertility after autotransplantation of cryopreserved whole ovaries. Live births achieved.
biological_outcomes.viability_metric: hormonal function, natural fertility, live births
biological_outcomes.notes: Demonstrates whole organ-scale cryopreservation and functional recovery, even with slow-freeze approach

formulations.notes: Not a vitrification protocol. Included for comparison: whole-organ cryopreservation success using traditional slow-freezing with relatively low CPA concentration.

formulations.name: Supercooling preservation (liver)

biological_outcomes.outcome: Extended liver preservation to 4 days (vs 24h standard cold storage). Transplanted livers supported life.
biological_outcomes.notes: Published in Nature Medicine 2014. Key advance: machine perfusion to deliver CPA uniformly, then supercooling (not freezing) to -6°C.

formulations.notes: This is NOT vitrification — it's subzero preservation without ice formation. Included because it represents an alternative preservation modality and uses CPAs differently.

formulations.name: High-throughput CPA screening panel
formulations.context: Systematic toxicity screening of individual CPAs and combinations

screening_results.notes: Good vitrifier, moderate toxicity. Most commonly used CPA.
Less toxic than DMSO at equimolar concentrations. Good permeability.
Least toxic of common penetrating CPAs. Slower membrane permeation.
Good balance of toxicity and permeability.
Highly effective vitrifier but significantly more toxic. EC50 is notably lower.
Excellent permeability but high systemic toxicity. Rarely used in tissue preservation.

formulations.notes: EC50 values are approximate, representing the concentration at which 50% cell death is observed in standardized assays. Actual values depend heavily on cell type, exposure time, and temperature. These give relative ordering.
formulations.confidence_note: Toxicity EC50 values are approximate and synthesized from multiple studies. The high-throughput screening paper provides more precise values for specific cell lines.
